close_reason: Replaced 5 literal 'vault' strings with BACKEND const
description:
lib.rs:73 defines 'const BACKEND: &str = "vault"' and uses it at lib.rs:87/92 (in map_http_status), 215/249 (in get). But lib.rs:165 (from_uri token error), 201 (get reqwest send error), 273 (put serde error), and 307 (put reqwest send error) hardcode the literal "vault" instead.

This is the kind of inconsistency that produces a class of small PRs from contributors fixing one site and missing the others. If the backend ever needs to be renamed (unlikely for vault, but the pattern matters across the workspace), the literal sites are silent footguns.

Fix: replace all "vault" literals with BACKEND. P3 because functionally equivalent today; the cost is contributor confusion when copying patterns to a new backend.
title: BACKEND constant defined but inconsistently used — three places use literal "vault"
